metadata.seed: Well, I. I've been doing heroin for a few months now, and I didn't think my parents knew, but I guess they. They found a needle in my room and, I mean, they knew what I was doing, and they. They told me that I have to start shaping up and I need to stop doing any kind of drugs because they said. They said the next time that they find any kind of drugs, then that's it, they're going to kick me out of the house.

patient.query: Well, I. I've been doing heroin for a few months now, and I didn't think my parents knew, but I guess they. They said the next time that they find any kind of drugs, then that's it, they're going to kick me out of the house.

triggers: parents told me to stop drugs
automatic_thoughts: I'll be kicked out of the house

patient.query: Yeah, it definitely did. I just felt so exposed, you know? Like, all the things I’ve been trying to hide just came crashing down. It’s hard to shake off that feeling of being judged, especially by them.

automatic_thoughts: things I’ve been trying to hide came crashing down

safety_check.response: It sounds really difficult to carry that weight, especially when you feel like you're being watched or judged. Can you tell me how that feeling affects your thoughts or emotions?

patient.query: Yeah, it was really overwhelming. I felt this heavy knot in my stomach, like I just wanted to disappear. It’s like all those moments of feeling unworthy just rushed back, and I couldn’t stop thinking about what they might be saying when I'm not around. It makes me second-guess myself a lot.

automatic_thoughts: I couldn't stop thinking about what they might be saying when I'm not around

neutral: Normal grief reaction to life event (finding)

patient.query: Yeah, it really is draining. I don't even know how I got to this point. Sometimes, it feels like I’m stuck in this loop of self-doubt, and I just can’t break free. I keep wondering if I’ll ever feel like I’m enough, you know?

I’ll never feel like I’m enough

patient.query: I guess it’s just this constant nagging feeling that I’m not doing enough, or that I should be handling things better. I see others seeming to have it all together, and I just feel...lost in comparison. There’s this fear that I’ll always fall short, and it feels like it just keeps building up until I can’t breathe.
